content.problemStatement:
Consider a permutation PPP of length NNN. We call a subarray of PPP good if it contains the value XXX.

Consider a set SSS containing maximum elements of all good subarrays of PPP.
Permutation PPP is called **beautiful** if SSS contains exactly KKK **distinct** values.

Given three integers NNN, XXX, and KKK, you need to find the number of beautiful permutations of length NNN. Since this number can be very large, output it modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.

Note: A subarray is obtained from an array by removing some elements (possibly none) from the beginning and some elements (possibly none) from the end of the array.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, XXX, and KKK - the length of the permutation, the pivot value XXX, and the number of different values the maximum element of the focused subarrays can take on.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the number of beautiful permutations modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 222 beautiful permutations:

[1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]. In this permutation, there are three subarrays that contain the pivot value 111: [1][1][1], [1,3][1, 3][1,3], and [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]. The maximum elements for these subarrays are 111, 333, and 333 respectively. So there are exactly 222 distinct values of maximum on these subarrays.
[2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1]. In this permutation, there are three subarrays that contain the pivot value 111: [1][1][1], [3,1][3, 1][3,1], and [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1]. The maximum elements for these subarrays are 111, 333, and 333 respectively. So there are exactly 222 distinct values of maximum on these subarrays.
